blocks.body: Past Simple: Forms and Use — V2 / -ed for finished actions in the past.

{"level":"A1–A2","intro":"In Past Simple, we use V2 or the -ed form to talk about a finished action in the past.","rules":[{"label":"Regular verbs","color":"emerald","text":"Many verbs add -ed.","example":"work → worked / play → played"},{"label":"Irregular verbs","color":"blue","text":"Some verbs have a special V2 form.","example":"go → went / see → saw / have → had"},{"label":"Finished time","color":"amber","text":"This tense often goes with words showing finished past time.","example":"yesterday, last night, in 2022"}]}

{"title":"1. Basic formula","intro":"In Past Simple, the form is the same for all subjects.","items":[{"label":"All subjects","title":"subject + V2 / -ed","subtitle":"I worked. / She visited. / They arrived."},{"label":"Regular verbs","title":"work → worked / live → lived","subtitle":"With regular verbs, we usually add -ed."},{"label":"Irregular verbs","title":"go → went / buy → bought","subtitle":"Irregular forms need to be learned."},{"label":"Time markers","title":"yesterday / last week / in 2021","subtitle":"These words often go with Past Simple."}]}

{"title":"2. When we use Past Simple","sections":[{"label":"Finished action","color":"emerald","description":"Use it for an action that happened and is complete.","examples":[{"en":"I cleaned my room yesterday.","ua":"A finished action in the past."},{"en":"She called me after class.","ua":"A finished event."}]},{"label":"Past fact or situation","color":"blue","description":"Talk about facts or situations that were true in the past.","examples":[{"en":"We lived in Lublin in 2020.","ua":"A past situation."},{"en":"He worked in a small shop last year.","ua":"A finished past period."}]},{"label":"Several actions in order","color":"amber","description":"Use it in a short story with finished steps one after another.","examples":[{"en":"I opened the window, sat down, and started to read.","ua":"A short sequence in the past."},{"en":"They arrived, said hello, and went inside.","ua":"One finished action followed another."}]}]}

{"title":"3. Quick form check","intro":"In an affirmative sentence, just use the past form of the verb.","rows":[{"en":"I watched a film yesterday.","ua":"watched","note":"regular verb + -ed"},{"en":"She visited her grandma last weekend.","ua":"visited","note":"regular verb + -ed"},{"en":"We went home early.","ua":"went","note":"irregular verb V2"},{"en":"They had lunch at noon.","ua":"had","note":"irregular verb V2"}],"warning":"Do not say I go yesterday or We did watched."}

{"title":"4. Common mistakes","items":[{"label":"Mistake 1","color":"rose","title":"Using V1 instead of the past form.","wrong":"❌ She watch TV last night.","right":"✅ She watched TV last night."},{"label":"Mistake 2","color":"amber","title":"Building an affirmative sentence with did.","wrong":"❌ We did played football yesterday.","right":"✅ We played football yesterday."},{"label":"Mistake 3","color":"sky","title":"Leaving an irregular verb in the base form.","wrong":"❌ He go to bed late.","right":"✅ He went to bed late."}]}

{"title":"5. Short summary","items":["An affirmative Past Simple sentence = subject + V2 / -ed.","The form is the same with I / you / he / she / it / we / they.","Regular verbs usually take -ed.","Irregular verbs have their own V2 form.","Use this tense for finished actions and events in the past."]}
